questions.questionText: A wave has a frequency of 50 Hz and wavelength of 4 m. What is its speed?
The frequency of a wave is 25 Hz. What is its period?
Which of the following is NOT a property shared by all types of waves?
questions.hint: Think about electromagnetic waves — what do they travel through in space?
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic waves (e.g. light, radio waves) can travel through a vacuum — they need no medium. All mechanical waves need a medium, but not all waves do.

questions.questionText: When a water wave moves from deep to shallow water, what happens to its wavelength?
questions.hint: Speed decreases in shallow water. Frequency stays the same. Use v = fλ.
questions.explanation: In shallow water, speed decreases. Since frequency is constant (v = fλ), wavelength must also decrease proportionally.

questions.questionText: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence is:
options: Greater than the angle of reflection
Less than the angle of reflection
Equal to the angle of reflection
Complementary to the angle of reflection
questions.hint: Both angles are measured from the normal to the surface.
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence always equals the angle of reflection. Both are measured from the normal (perpendicular) to the reflecting surface.

questions.questionText: Diffraction of waves is most pronounced when:
options: The gap is much larger than the wavelength
The gap is comparable to or smaller than the wavelength
The wave frequency is very high
questions.hint: Compare the size of the gap with the size of one wave cycle.
questions.explanation: When the gap size ≈ wavelength, diffraction is maximum and waves spread significantly. If the gap is much larger, waves pass through with little spreading.

questions.questionText: Constructive interference occurs when two waves meet:
options: Exactly out of phase (half wavelength path difference)
Exactly in phase (whole wavelength path difference)
At right angles to each other
questions.hint: Constructive means they add up — they must be in step with each other.
questions.explanation: When two waves are in phase (path difference = 0, λ, 2λ...), their crests align and amplitudes add — producing maximum constructive interference.

questions.questionText: Which statement correctly describes sound and light waves?
options: Sound is transverse, light is longitudinal
Sound is longitudinal, light is transverse
questions.hint: Can light travel through a vacuum? Can sound?
questions.explanation: Sound is a mechanical longitudinal wave (particles vibrate along the direction of travel). Light is an electromagnetic transverse wave (oscillations perpendicular to travel direction).

questions.questionText: The speed of light in glass is 2×10⁸ m/s. What is the refractive index of glass? (c = 3×10⁸ m/s)
questions.explanation: n = c/v = (3×10⁸) / (2×10⁸) = 1.5. A refractive index greater than 1 means light slows down in the medium.

options: A single wave reflecting off a hard barrier
Two waves of different frequencies travelling together
Two identical waves travelling in opposite directions
Diffraction of waves through a narrow slit
questions.hint: Think about what happens when two identical waves collide head-on.
questions.explanation: When two waves of equal frequency and amplitude travel in opposite directions, their superposition creates a standing wave with fixed nodes and antinodes.
